rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. Plaintiffs demonstrated good cause for the court to exercise its discretion to allow plaintiffs leave to amend pursuant to C.C.P. 473(a)(1). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are continued on the court¿s own motion to April 11, 2012 to allow for supplemental briefing. Supplemental briefing is limited to a discussion of the recent Supreme Court of California decision in O¿Neil v. Crane Co. (2012 WL 88533) and its impact, if any, on this motion. Supplemental opposition, if any, must be filed and served by March 22, 2012. Supplemental reply, if any, must be filed and served by April 3, 3012. If a hearing is requested to contest this tentative ruling, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD AMANTITE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 25, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence demonstrating that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to defendant¿s 2010 supplemental standard interrogatory responses are sustained. Defendant¿s alternate motion for judgment on the pleadings relating to plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
